title: Bridge Essay: Mentalizing during social InterAction: A four component model.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.

**Mentalizing during social InterAction: Afour component model.**. Cortex.. PMID: 32092493. DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2019.12.031. Mentalizing, conventionally defined as the process in which we infer the inner thoughts and intentions of others, is afundamental component of human social cognition. Yet its role, and the nuanced layers involved, in real world social interaction are rarely discussed. To account for this lack of theory, we propose the interactive mentalizing theory (IMT) -to emphasize the role of metacognition in different mentalizing components.
